title: Unify anniversary calculation logic and add anniversary-type filtering on Dashboard

requirements.text: Unify anniversary-year math in the backend so Dashboard anniversaries use the exact same rule as the Kartoteka parishioner card: anniversaryNumber must be computed as (targetYear - eventYear) (NO +1 offset), based strictly on the corresponding single sacrament year field (baptismYear/marriageYear/funeralYear). Do not return anniversaries where anniversaryNumber is 0 (i.e., when targetYear == eventYear).

quotes: both views count anniversaries in an identical way: always based on one field, with the same logic “current year – event year”.
if baptism was for example in 2016 then in current year 2026 we have exactly the 10th baptism anniversary

acceptanceCriteria: For a parishioner with baptismYear = 2016, the backend anniversaries response for targetYear = 2026 returns anniversaryNumber = 10 for the baptism anniversary entry.
For targetYear equal to the eventYear (e.g., baptismYear = 2026, targetYear = 2026), the backend does not include that anniversary entry in the results.
Anniversary entries continue to be sourced only from the relevant year field for each type (baptismYear, marriageYear, funeralYear), not from any other derived or date-like fields.

requirements.text: Add backend support for filtering anniversaries by type for both paginated and PDF-export endpoints, so the frontend can request only: baptism, marriage, or funeral anniversaries for a given target year. The filtering must apply before pagination and must also affect totalCount/pageCount and PDF export contents.

quotes: On the Dashboard add the ability to filter anniversaries by: baptism, marriage and funeral.

acceptanceCriteria: The paginated anniversaries API can be called with an optional anniversary type filter and returns only matching anniversary entries.
When a filter is applied, totalCount and pageCount reflect the filtered dataset (not the unfiltered one).
The PDF export anniversaries API can be called with the same optional filter and returns only matching anniversary entries.

requirements.text: Update the Dashboard anniversaries section to add a UI control that filters the anniversaries list by type (All, Baptism, Marriage, Funeral). The filter must work together with the existing year selector and pagination (changing year or filter resets to page 1). The displayed anniversary numbers must match Kartoteka card logic (current year minus event year).

quotes: On the Dashboard add the ability to filter anniversaries by: baptism, marriage and funeral.
you can take inspiration from the Kartoteka card — it shows it nicely there

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard provides a filter control with options: All, Baptism, Marriage, Funeral.
Selecting a filter updates the list to show only the selected anniversary type (or all types when 'All' is selected).
Pagination counts and navigation reflect the filtered dataset (no empty pages caused by client-side-only filtering).
For a 2016 baptism and selectedYear = 2026, Dashboard displays '10th anniversary' (i.e., number 10), matching the Kartoteka card logic.

requirements.text: Update the Dashboard anniversaries PDF export to respect the currently selected anniversary type filter (All/Baptism/Marriage/Funeral) and selected year, generating a document that contains only the filtered anniversary entries.

quotes: On the Dashboard add the ability to filter anniversaries by: baptism, marriage and funeral.

acceptanceCriteria: When the Dashboard filter is set to a specific type (e.g., Marriage), the exported PDF includes only Marriage anniversaries for the selected year.
When the Dashboard filter is set to All, the exported PDF includes all anniversary types for the selected year.

constraints: Keep backend as a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo (no additional backend services).
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui or other frontend immutable paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT; compose existing UI components instead.
nonGoals: Do not change the Kartoteka parishioner-card selection priority logic (funeral > marriage > baptism); only ensure the year-difference math matches across views.
Do not add new anniversary types beyond baptism, marriage, and funeral.
Do not introduce month/day-based anniversary calculations; anniversaries remain year-based only.
